methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Agile Robots is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company seeking valuation following ~$384M in total capital raised. The peer universe comprises nine same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies: one public entity (277810.KQ) and eight private firms including Hadrian, 1X Technologies, Exotec, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Nimble Robotics, and Divergent.

Peer-median analysis applies directly to this stage and sector. The median valuation across the nine-company comp set is $1.6B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. Notable peers at comparable or adjacent stages include Hadrian at $1.6B (Series C), Dexterity at $1.6B (Series B), Bedrock Robotics at $1.8B (Series B), and Exotec at $2.0B (Series D). This method yielded an implied valuation of $1.6B.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to one applicable method, supports a valuation range of $1.4B to $1.8B, with a median of $1.6B. This aligns with the target's stated >$1B current valuation. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion within the peer set ($300M–$10.0B), which reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity, commercialization progress, and capital efficiency across robotics vendors. The preponderance of private comparables limits bench-marking rigor relative to public market data. Capital intensity and path to profitability remain material variables not fully captured in peer multiples.
